document.title: Living Folders Orientation
document.purpose: Orient humans and LLMs to the first working form of Living Folders.

keystone: A directory should be allowed to present itself according to its purpose.
The filesystem is already a database, a workspace, a map, and a control surface, but ordinary browsers mostly show it as a list.

present-form.identity: A dependency-free Python and Tkinter semantic lens over one ordinary directory at a time.
present-form.boundary: Living Folders does not import, relocate, own, or hide the user's files.
present-form.constitution: A directory may describe itself through .living-folder.json. Two earlier names, .directory-role.json and .decorator.json, are also recognized.
portrait: detected runnable files and declared actions
raw files as a fallback material list
present-form.execution-safety: Commands are visible but disabled until the user explicitly trusts runnable code in the open folder. Commands run with that directory as their current working directory.

interpretive-modes.project-root: Emphasize code areas, project signals, and local commands.
interpretive-modes.factory: Emphasize inputs, outputs, stages, and production affordances.
interpretive-modes.ruin: Emphasize age, warning, neglect, and historical truth.
interpretive-modes.workbench: Emphasize active material, recency, and tools.

design-discipline: External JSON is normalized once at the castle gate.
The GUI consumes one canonical folder portrait.
Observation is immediate; execution requires trust.
Unknown roles remain usable instead of being rejected.
The application should stay small enough to understand at a glance.
